counter_argument: Exploit is conditional on an unverified Codex parser-leniency differential (only 0.143.0 source-verified; min supported 0.134.0); the whole-envelope backstop IS observed to drop unrecognized *** directives, so the code weakness is certain even if reachability is not.
rationale: Fail-closed opaque should trip per-file / on ANY unrecognized '*** ' directive inside an envelope, not only when zero ops parse. Cheap defensive hardening at a trust boundary.

counter_argument: Mirrors an already-accepted Claude-side gap (MCP write tools escape the Write/Edit matchers under the ADR-0010 cooperative-agent trust model); not branch-introduced, but the dual-host work widens the surface and Codex MCP servers are user-addable.
rationale: security-controls.md must state MCP-tool writes explicitly in- or out-of-scope for the write gate on Codex; not a clear code fix, a boundary-scope decision.

Remove the dead host param from all 20 run() entries, or wire it so run(host) truly injects; today it silently runs against the disk-loaded _host.py, a DI-that-isnt footgun that would make cross-host contract tests exercise the wrong host.

counter_argument: Manual sync-core.py discipline currently holds and the copies are byte-identical today; but ADR-0011 itself calls the CI byte-identity/parity jobs load-bearing and never optional, and their total absence means a core edit ships stale vendored copies (v1 death mode) with a green build.
rationale: Add to CI: sync-core.py --check job, core/** and plugins/ca-codex/** path filters, a ca-codex version-bump gate, and the ca-codex hook-suite run. Systemic, amplifies every other drift risk.

Wire test_codex_adapter.py (the trust-boundary parser + cross-host parity proof) into CI; today it has zero callers and docs/parity.md points at it as the parity proof that never runs. Same PR as architecture-002.

counter_argument: Triggers only on a broken/partial ca-codex _host.py (syntax error, corrupt/absent file, import shadow); a correct install loads CodexHost fine, so this is an install/upgrade-failure fail-open, not a steady-state hole. Still high: the consequence is total silent un-enforcement of the write gate on Codex with zero breadcrumb, and the base-class-IS-Claude asymmetry hides it on the host where it is harmless.
rationale: load_host() must fail CLOSED (or emit a loud breadcrumb + refuse foreign payloads) when a _host.py is PRESENT but fails to load, instead of silently returning the Claude default Host(). The fallback is only safe when the intended host actually IS Claude.

Gate the vendored payload / capability-gate the unreachable entries so ca-codex does not ship ~15 non-registered enforcement scripts as trust-surface bloat, and fix the manifest first-run pointer (points at a /ca-init that ca-codex does not ship).
Payload-cwd leg of Host.project_root is dead in production (no call site passes a payload); resolve the seam root-signal design. Grouped with reliability-005.

counter_argument: Atomic sibling-temp + os.replace prevents torn files, so this is lost-update / weak-attribution, not corruption; and concurrent same-host sessions already had the exposure. But ADR-0011 newly advertises concurrent Claude+Codex on one store as the designed configuration, and the audit trail (conflict-hierarchy level 1) cannot attribute an event to a host.
rationale: ADR-grade: define the coordination contract for two hosts sharing one .codearbiter/ store: locking/CAS on read-modify-write state, and host= attribution in gate-events.log/overrides.log. Resolve via /ca:adr. Grouped with reliability-004 and reliability-007.

pre-edit.py branches on raw native tool names (MultiEdit/NotebookEdit) inside the host-neutral core, held safe only by ca-codex non-registration convention; route through normalize_tool categories so a third host that registers it cannot silently lose H-05/H-18.
_hooklib.py 773 LOC / six concern clusters / ~20 consumers: real accretion debt but no correctness impact; below the low confidence gate, parked as investigate rather than filed.

ORCHESTRATOR.md is hand-duplicated across the two plugins with NO sync mechanism (the core/surface/ + build-surface.py named in ADR-0011 do not exist); a ca-side edit silently ships divergent governance to Codex. Build or check-gate the markdown surface. Adjacent to group-ci-vendoring.
doctor.py hard-codes .claude-plugin/plugin.json so every healthy ca-codex install reports UNHEALTHY/exit 1 (ca-codex ships .codex-plugin/). Host-aware manifest path. Grouped with reliability-002.
_updatelib.installed_version reads the same .claude-plugin path so the update-notifier silently never fires on Codex. Same host-aware manifest-path fix. Grouped with reliability-001.
Same root cause as architecture-004 (load_host silent Claude-default fallback fail-opens the Codex write gate).

counter_argument: Crash-atomic replace means no torn file; lost-update needs a genuine concurrent write to open-tasks.md. But dual-host sharing is the branch headline configuration, so two /ca:task sessions interleaving silently drop a board update or mint a duplicate dotted ID.
rationale: Concrete instance of the group-shared-store contract: lock/CAS the board read-modify-write. Grouped with architecture-007.

CodexHost.project_root documented primary signal (payload cwd) is unreachable AND returns session cwd verbatim (subdir -> wrong root); wiring it as designed makes subdir Codex sessions dormant. Grouped with architecture-006.

prune-transcript staleness_check resolves root from raw payload cwd, bypassing the host seam entirely; a subdir-cwd session silently loses the CONFIRM-09 audit-staleness WARN. Route through the seam project_root.

SessionStart unconditionally clears the repo-global dev-active marker and logs a synthetic DEV: exit, clobbering a concurrent host live /dev session and writing a false audit close. Part of the group-shared-store repo-global-vs-session-local contract.

counter_argument: Confidence tempered because Codex own hook-failure semantics (fail-open vs surfaced error) are not verifiable from this repo; but the dual-registration parity fix is unambiguously correct and free (ca already does it), so it is a keep regardless of which way Codex fails.
rationale: ca-codex hooks.json registers each hook ONCE; mirror ca dual python3/python interpreter registration so a Store-stub/py3-missing box does not silently fail every Codex gate open.

Dual-host git-hook shims embed the last-writing plugin absolute enforcer path and are fail-open when it is missing; uninstalling whichever plugin last wrote them (e.g. the BETA ca-codex) dangles the path and silently unwires the #161 git-level backstop for BOTH hosts. Make the shim resolve either install or fail closed.

counter_argument: ca-codex is BETA and not yet released, so no release has silently mis-shipped it yet; but the sanctioned release path is the only one, and it has no ca-codex job at all (unlike the ca-sandbox twin pattern), so the first release ships nothing for the new host.
rationale: release.yml: add a ca-codex-v* publish job mirroring the ca-sandbox release-gate pattern (manifest/tag/CHANGELOG for plugins/ca-codex). Theme: group-ci-vendoring.

counter_argument: Confidence shares reliability-008's caveat — Codex's own hook-failure semantics are unverified — but the matrix-coverage GAP is certain: test_hooks_cold_install.py is hardcoded to plugins/ca and never loads ca-codex's differently-shaped single-entry hooks.json.
rationale: Extend the cold-install matrix to ca-codex, and give ca-codex the STUB-python3 fallback ca already has (ties to reliability-008's dual-registration fix). Theme: group-ci-vendoring + group-cold-install.

check_license_consistency.py MANIFESTS list omits plugins/ca-codex/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, so the always-on license-consistency CI job never checks the new plugin — add it to the list. Theme: group-ci-vendoring.

No fixture exercises a partially-recognized apply_patch envelope (recognized op + dropped unrecognized directive) — the test deliverable that rides appsec-001's per-directive fail-closed fix. Grouped with the parser gap.
CodexHost.iter_file_ops' non-patch-tool defensive fallback branch (_host.py:243-247) is untested; add a fixture for an mcp__*/OTHER payload carrying file_path.

counter_argument: The severity is inherited from the code defect it guards (the load_host fail-open, architecture-004); as a pure test gap in isolation it is medium, but the untested path IS the enforcement-critical silent-bypass path, so it files high and should land in the same PR as the code fix.
rationale: Add a test for hostapi.load_host()'s except-and-fall-back path — currently zero coverage anywhere (test_codex_adapter.py always loads _host.py directly, never through load_host's try/except). Rides the architecture-004 fix.

session-start.py's host.has_statusline gate is asserted as a flag value but never exercised through main()'s actual skip behavior under a Codex-shaped host; add an end-to-end main() test with has_statusline=False.

counter_argument: Adding host= to the log lines is a small concrete fix, not itself an ADR-grade choice; but it is the audit-completeness half of the shared-store contract (architecture-007), so it files under that group as the attribution requirement.
rationale: gate-events.log/overrides.log must record get_host().name (available at every _log_gate_event/block/remind/warn call site) so two hosts' entries in one shared trail are attributable. Grouped with the shared-store contract.
Same defect as architecture-004 from the observability angle — load_host swallows a broken _host.py with zero breadcrumb; the fix must emit a signal, not just fail closed.
Same defect as reliability-002 — update notifier dark on Codex via the wrong manifest path; folds into group-manifest-path.

Neither session-start.py's startup banner nor doctor.py's report prints the resolved host.name, so a dormant Codex install is indistinguishable from a working one at the one place a maintainer looks; surface host.name in both. Reinforces the group-load-host-failopen signal gap.

Every Codex hook invocation spawns git rev-parse because project_root() is called with no payload before stdin is parsed — the payload-cwd fast path is structurally unreachable. Same root as the dead payload-leg design. Grouped with the project-root seam.
Same root as architecture-001 (dead run(host) param) — the perf consequence is _host.py exec-loaded twice per invocation. Fixing the dead param fixes this.

Host.project_root() is not memoized at process level, so every BLOCK/REMIND/WARN re-resolves it, multiplying performance-001's Codex subprocess spawn within one call; memoize as part of the project-root seam fix.

counter_argument: Lens rated critical and reproduced directly (observed): base Host().iter_file_ops on a Codex apply_patch payload yields file_path='' so classify_protected skips H-18/H-19/H-05/H-11. Held at high not critical because the trigger is a broken/failed _host.py load (install/upgrade defect), not attacker-reachable input.
rationale: Duplicate of architecture-004; the reproduction raises confidence in that anchor and the fix MUST fail closed.
